regulatory_context.value: Healthcare service providers must prove necessary language skills, and medical practitioners can use B2/INLL proof routes in Luxembourg.

training_support_route.value: Luxembourg company-training co-funding exists at 15%, with a 35% wage-cost uplift for qualifying participants.

target_audience_summary.value: Healthcare employers and frontline staff in Luxembourg who need practical Luxembourgish for patient-facing work.
target_audience_summary.omission_reason: Useful synthesis, but still partly inferential and not safe as default dashboard truth.

market_evidence_summary.omission_reason: Derived SEMrush market-evidence layer for prioritization and further research. Keep it out of customer-facing copy until translated into route-safe product language.

semrush_anchor_evidence.omission_reason: Derived SEMrush market-evidence layer for prioritization and further research. Keep it out of customer-facing copy until translated into route-safe product language.

independent_operator_examples.name: LuxLessons
independent_operator_examples.evidence: Small Luxembourgish training provider already positioned publicly in the same local language and prep market.

independent_operator_examples.name: Allingua
independent_operator_examples.evidence: Commercial Luxembourg language provider with professional-language positioning relevant to healthcare workers.

independent_operator_examples.name: Prolingua
independent_operator_examples.evidence: Established private school covering overlapping workplace-language needs.

pricing_or_offer_proof: Current proof is mainly presence of paid Luxembourgish providers; no clean healthcare-specific public price signal was captured.
value.social_funnel_signal: Facebook group search shows the same Luxembourgish-learning community layer (`Luxembourgish learning`, `Tandem Luxembourg`, `Luxembourgish coach/language exchange partner`), but no healthcare-specific community or operator funnel surfaced. The social layer supports underlying demand, not the medical wedge by itself.

segment_weighting.rationale: B2B product. Operator proof relevance: medium. Social funnel relevance: low. Confirmed independent operators increase the weight. Visible Facebook/community residue is treated as supporting but low-weight evidence.

value.takeaway: There is real paid supply in adjacent Luxembourgish training, so the underlying problem is not hypothetical.
value.why_not_stronger: Current operator proof is adjacent Luxembourgish-route demand, not a clean healthcare-specific operator cluster.

independent_operator_summary.omission_reason: Derived independent-operator signal for prioritization and further market research. Keep it out of customer-facing copy until translated into product-safe language.

product_delivery_model.omission_reason: Standalone course vs compliance support vs broader workplace language course remains a product-design decision, not a public fact.

product_accreditation_route.omission_reason: No public evidence was found that this product itself has a dedicated certification or accreditation route.

impact_outcome_claim.omission_reason: No direct external outcome evidence was found for patient communication, safety, or satisfaction.
